messages.content: What is true about solubility and temperature? Solubility decreases as temperature increases, solubility increases as temperature decreases, solubility increases as temperature increases or solubility stay the same as temperature increases? which one is correct?
Solubility decreases as temperature increases.
Soap remove grease from clothes because grease is insoluble in soap, soap is not soluble in water, grease is soluble in water, or grease is soluble in soap and water solution? which one is correct?
Grease is soluble in soap and water solution.
Which is a mechanical mixture? gold, milk, air or chocoalte chip cookie dough?
Chocolate chip cookie dough is a mechanical mixture because it consists of two or more substances that are physically mixed together but not chemically bonded.
Which of the following is a solution? hydrogen orange juice with pulp, syrup or sand and water?

Orange juice with pulp is not a solution because it is a mechanical mixture. Syrup is not a solution because it is a homogeneous mixture, but its components are not uniformly dispersed at the molecular level. Sand and water is not a solution because sand particles do not dissolve in water. 

Therefore, the only option left is hydrogen, which when dissolved in water can form a solution called hydrogen water.
Which process is used to collect salt from salt-water distillation, filtration, crystallization or mechanical separation? Which both are right?

Crystallization is the process used to collect salt from salt-water. It involves evaporating the water from the salt solution to the point where the salt begins to crystallize and then collecting the crystals that are left behind. 

However, filtration and mechanical separation can also be used in the initial stages of salt production to separate out larger impurities from the salt water or brine solution.
You can recognize a solution because you can see particles in it, it is always a solid, you can see only one type of substance or it is always colourless? Which one is correct?

You can recognize a solution because you can only see one type of substance, meaning that it is a homogeneous mixture of two or more components that are uniformly dispersed at the molecular level. Unlike suspensions, solutions do not have visible particles that can be seen with the naked eye, and solutions can be solids, liquids, or gases. The color of a solution can vary depending on its composition and concentration. Therefore, the correct option is "you can see only one type of substance."
The substance that will dissolve is concertrated, soluble, filtered or insouble?

The substance that will dissolve is "soluble." The term soluble refers to a substance's ability to dissolve in a solvent (such as water). A substance that is soluble will dissolve in the solvent to form a homogeneous mixture, while a substance that is insoluble will not dissolve and may form a separate layer or sediment at the bottom of the container.
Which substance is soluble in water? Gasoline, oil, petroleum jelly, or salt?
Out of the given options, only salt is soluble in water.
Which substance is insoluble in water? sugar, baking soda, salt or petroleum jelly?
Petroleum jelly is insoluble in water.
A window screen can be used to separate a solution, bacteria from water, a mechanical mixture or a solute from a solvent?

A window screen can be used to separate a mechanical mixture, which consists of two or more substances that are physically mixed together but not chemically bonded. A window screen can separate larger particles or components of the mixture from smaller ones, for example, separating gravel from sand, or filtering larger sediment from water in a fish tank. 

It would not be useful for separating a solution, which is a homogeneous mixture of two or more substances that are evenly distributed at a molecular level. It would also not be useful for separating bacteria from water or a solute from a solvent. For these purposes, other techniques such as filtration, centrifugation, or distillation would be more appropriate.
What substance in water purification system sticks to tiny dirt particles, bacteria, & viruses? Alum, chlorine, sand or flourine?

Alum is the substance in water purification systems that sticks to tiny dirt particles, bacteria, and viruses. Alum is a coagulant that is added to water to bind together the impurities and make them easier to remove. Once the impurities have clumped together, they can be easily removed through a process called sedimentation or filtration. The other options, chlorine, sand, and fluorine, are not typically used for this purpose in water purification systems. Chlorine is typically used as a disinfectant, sand is used for physical filtration, and fluorine is not typically used in water treatment.
A mixture of iron filings and sand could best be separated using distillation, magnetic, separation, crystallization or a filter?

A mixture of iron filings and sand could best be separated using magnetic separation. Iron is magnetic whereas sand is not, so a magnet can be used to attract the iron filings from the mixture. Separating the mixture with a filter, crystallization, or distillation would not be effective in this case.
Sugar dissolves in water because it is insoluble, solid, soluble or saturated?

Sugar dissolves in water because it is "soluble." Sugar is a polar compound with a hydroxyl group and aldehyde or ketone group. Water is also a polar solvent. When sugar is added to water, the polar water molecules surround the sugar molecules and break apart the sugar crystals by pulling the individual sugar molecules away from the crystal surface and dissolving them. The sugar molecules become incorporated into the water molecules and the mixture becomes a homogeneous solution. So, sugar is soluble in water.
The chemical name for common table salt is calcium carbonate, potassium nitrate, sodium chloride, or copper sulfate?
The chemical name for common table salt is "sodium chloride."
